channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lok Sabha Expansion and Delimitation: Modi Government's Plan and Potential Impact
item.description: The Modi government is planning a significant overhaul of the Lok Sabha, aiming to increase its size from 543 to 850 seats, while simultaneously implementing the Women's Reservation Act. This ambitiou...

item.title: Tanzania Considers Using Academic Certificates as Loan Collateral to Support Youth Entrepreneurs
item.description: Tanzania’s Prime Minister, Mwigulu Nchemba, recently announced that the government is working on a legal framework allowing graduates to use their academic certificates as collateral for loans. This m...

item.title: OPP concludes investigation into alleged incidents at Ontario charity golf tournament with no charges
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police have confirmed that no charges will be issued following an investigation into allegations of sexual acts at the Poppa Pie’s Adventures Golf Tournament held on August 2, 2025,...

item.title: Centre proposes major delimitation plan to expand Lok Sabha to 850 seats based on 2011 census
item.description: The Union government has introduced a set of bills ahead of a special session of Parliament proposing significant changes to India’s electoral structure. Central to the proposal is the Constitution 13...

item.title: KRA Seeks New Commissioner General After Wattanga’s Appointment to South Africa
item.description: The Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) has officially announced a vacancy for the role of Commissioner General, following the departure of Humphrey Wattanga. In a notice issued on Monday, the Authority exp...

item.title: IMF urges cautious spending as Middle East conflict drives inflation and recession risks
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has warned Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers against using the upcoming federal budget to deliver broad cost-of-living relief that could worsen inflation, as the ...

item.title: Hattie Dynevor and Callum Doyle enjoy a relaxed outing in Cheshire
item.description: Hattie Dynevor, 22, known for her role in the BBC series Waterloo Road, was seen enjoying a peaceful walk in Cheshire with her boyfriend, professional footballer Callum Doyle, also 22. The couple appe...

item.title: Science Corp. Plans First Human Trial of Biohybrid Brain Sensor
item.description: Science Corporation, the startup founded by former Neuralink president Max Hodak, is preparing to conduct its first human trial of a biohybrid brain-computer interface. The company has recruited Dr. M...

item.title: Australia lags in electric vehicle adoption despite clear cost and environmental benefits
item.description: Australia is trailing behind other nations in adopting electric vehicles (EVs), despite the growing evidence that switching from petrol and diesel cars can offer substantial savings and environmental ...

item.title: Man diagnosed with HPV-related throat cancer after persistent sore throat
item.description: Jeff Bradford, a 62-year-old father from Moray, Scotland, was diagnosed with stage three oropharyngeal cancer after initially attributing a persistent sore throat to dust exposure during home renovati...

item.title: How Bookmakers Are Preparing for Major Sports Events in 2026
item.description: The year 2026 promises to be very busy for sports fans and sports betting alike. Major events such as the FIFA World Cup, a highly competitive NBA season, Formula 1 with new unpredictable rules, and t...

item.title: Quebec’s new premier faces uphill battle but may curb party’s divisive policies
item.description: Christine Fréchette is set to become Quebec’s next premier, though her tenure may be short-lived due to the Coalition Avenir Québec’s (CAQ) low polling at roughly 10 per cent. Despite the bleak outloo...

item.title: Kesha Neve wins Australian Idol 2026, earning $100,000 and recording deal to launch music career

item.title: Omtatah Raises Concern Over Return of Political Violence Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Busia Senator Okiya Omtatah has come out strongly to warn Kenyans about the possible return of politically sponsored violence as the country moves closer to the 2027 General Election. Speaking while r...

item.title: President Tinubu Calls for Public Trust in Revenue Service as Reforms Continue
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don charge the Nigeria Revenue Service to make sure people trust dem again, especially now things dey change for how we collect tax. Him talk this one while him dey commission di...

item.title: Woman known as ‘Baby Jessica’ arrested in Texas on domestic violence charge
item.description: Jessica McClure Morales, widely known as “Baby Jessica”, has been arrested in Texas on suspicion of domestic violence following a reported disturbance at her home in Midland County. The 40-year-old wo...

item.title: Experiencing Coachella Through 18 Hours of Vertical Livestream: The Ups and Downs
item.description: Boone Ashworth spent an entire Coachella weekend watching the festival exclusively through vertical video on his phone. Coachella’s revamped vertical livestream, available on YouTube with 4K quality a...

item.title: Exploring the Artistic Typography of Matt W. Moore
item.description: Matt W. Moore’s latest series, Wordplay, reimagines typography as a tangible, sculptural medium rather than just a method of communication. Using a striking blue color palette, Moore experiments with ...

item.title: Domestic Workers in Noida's Sector 121 Protest for Wage Hike and Better Working Conditions
item.description: In Noida's Sector 121, domestic workers employed in high-profile residential complexes staged a protest demanding a raise in their salaries and improved working conditions. The demonstrators highlight...
